meaning: n.斗蓬,覆盖物,壁炉架v.披风,覆盖
examples: Reservoirs of this hot rock collect in the base of the mantle.
Because of its proximity to Earth's core, the rock at the base of the mantle is much hotter than rock in the upper mantle.
The "mantle plume" thus formed, once established, continues to channel hot material from the mantle base until the reservoir is emptied.
Collins is letting it known that he is willing to take on that mantle.
The inquest heard Mr Mantle drowned after falling and hitting his head, which knocked him unconscious.
